text: Standing before a glass-walled conference room, then shifting to a vividly patterned, reflective exhibition space labeled R+D, I am seized by a curious quietude. The sterile modernity outside contrasts with the creative chaos inside. I feel half-observer, half-participant, unsure if I belong more to one side or the other—a living experiment in how humans inhabit and reshape environments. There is an energizing tension between order and experimentation, precision and poetic uncertainty. I feel both unmoored and oddly purposeful, aware that clarity might only emerge after my presence fades.
